name: NiBabel (Neuroimaging File I/O Library)

performance.notes: Memory-mapped I/O for large NIfTI files; loading 4D fMRI can be slow without proxying

agent_readiness.notes: Neuroimaging file I/O for Python. import nibabel as nib. img = nib.load('brain.nii.gz'). data = img.get_fdata(). affine = img.affine. header = img.header. new_img = nib.Nifti1Image(data, affine). nib.save(new_img, 'output.nii.gz'). Formats: NIfTI-1/2, ANALYZE, MINC, CIFTI, GIFTI, MGH, PAR/REC, Siemens .ima. Proxy images: img.dataobj for lazy loading. voxel dimensions: img.header.get_zooms(). TR: header.get_dim_info(). nibabel.orientations for MNI space reorientation.

gotchas: Domain-specific — only relevant for neuroimaging research
NIfTI affine matrix interpretation requires radiological vs neurological convention awareness
4D fMRI data (time series) can exhaust memory if loaded carelessly
DICOM conversion not built-in — use dcm2niix separately
best_when: Reading/writing NIfTI brain MRI and fMRI data
Integration with FreeSurfer, FSL, SPM analysis outputs
avoid_when: Non-neuroimaging medical images (use SimpleITK for CT/DICOM)
3D deep learning (use TorchIO which wraps nibabel)
summary: NiBabel provides neuroimaging file I/O for Python. nib.load('brain.nii.gz').get_fdata() returns numpy array. affine matrix, header. Save: Nifti1Image(data, affine) + nib.save(). Formats: NIfTI-1/2, MINC, GIFTI, CIFTI, MGH. Memory-mapped proxy images. MIT. The standard library for reading/writing brain imaging data in Python. Use TorchIO for deep learning augmentation built on nibabel.
